title: LINDO VOO de DRONE na PATAGÔNIA ARGENTINA! DJI MINI 4 PRO
creator: Rudy WT (@Rudy-WT)
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the Patagonia-style dramatic-granite-peak-and-steppe-landscape subgenre bucket (not previously represented anywhere in this pack; confirmed by listing data/reference_pack/exemplars/ at the start of this session -- 32 existing files, none Patagonia-related). A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/YBMgzLv4kY8); per this task's step-1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via two independent methods. First, YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/YBMgzLv4kY8&format=json), which returned title="LINDO VOO de DRONE na PATAGÔNIA ARGENTINA! DJI MINI 4 PRO", author_name="Rudy WT ", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@Rudy-WT", provider_name="YouTube", and a portrait embed (height=200, width=113) -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator claims (note the accented "PATAGÔNIA" is reproduced verbatim from the oEmbed response, correcting the candidate's unaccented "PATAGONIA"). Second, this session independently re-ran yt-dlp itself, metadata-only (--skip-download --no-write-thumbnail --no-write-info-json, no video/thumbnail file ever written to disk), against the same URL and got: uploader="Rudy WT", upload_date=20250303, duration=90 (duration_string "1:30"), width=1080, height=1920 (vertical), view_count=1002, license=NA (empty), fulltitle matching the oEmbed title exactly -- independently corroborating every field the candidate's own prior yt-dlp run had reported (creator, upload date, 90s duration, vertical orientation, view count, absence of a CC license marking), from a fresh extraction in this session rather than re-quoting the candidate's numbers. This session also ran a WebSearch ("Rudy WT" drone Patagonia Argentina channel) and a WebFetch of https://www.youtube.com/@Rudy-WT: the WebSearch surfaced only that Rudy WT is a YouTube channel covering cameras/drones/equipment reviews and audiovisual tutorials generally, with no reputation signal (follower count, brand roster, press coverage, awards) specific to drone cinematography quality; the WebFetch of the channel page returned no usable bio/subscriber/equipment content (fetch tool limitation, not a finding of absence). `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': both this session's own yt-dlp run and the candidate's prior one found the license field empty/NA, and no explicit Creative Commons marking was found anywhere -- per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video/thumbnail file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is set to 90.0 (matching "1:30"/90s from two independent yt-dlp extractions -- the candidate's original run and this session's fresh re-run, both agreeing exactly), notwithstanding a real discrepancy flagged by this task's supplied editorial-review step: a live Playwright playback session reported a measured HTMLMediaElement.duration of 54.041s for the same URL. This record resolves that conflict in favor of the yt-dlp figure rather than leaving `duration` null (contrast this pack's pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json precedent, where duration was left null because its two live-playback readings contradicted each other with no third, independently-agreeing measurement to break the tie): here, two independent yt-dlp extractions agree exactly (90s / "1:30"), whereas only a single live-playback DOM read gives the outlier 54.041s figure, and this exact pack has repeatedly documented YouTube's Shorts feed silently auto-advancing the active <video> element to an unrelated clip during in-page script execution (see pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json and dereksturman-west-desert-utah-sand-dunes.json's editorial_style notes for two more instances of this exact failure mode), making a single unconfirmed live-DOM reading the less reliable of the two methods in this specific pack's track record. The discrepancy itself is disclosed here rather than silently discarded. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual creator's own Shorts upload with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.88 range: sharpness=0.74 -- at parity with this pack's instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json (0.74), both cases resting on the same kind of signal: a specific, named, reasonably capable consumer drone model disclosed in the title itself (DJI Mini 4 Pro here, comparable to that record's #mavicair2s hashtag) rather than an inference from genre alone -- but not lifted further, since no resolution, bitrate, or color-profile claim beyond the drone model name itself was found, and the channel's own reputation signal (equipment-review/tutorial channel, no drone-cinematography-specific press or following found) is materially weaker than the professionally-corroborated creators scored higher in this pack (e.g. matthiasdangl-maldives-overwater-lagoon-fpv.json's 0.79, built on a confirmed multi-brand client roster and DJI's own channel featuring the creator's work). exposure=0.72 -- this pack's un-curated-personal-account floor (matching wanderpeaks-langhe-autumn-vineyards.json and dereksturman-west-desert-utah-sand-dunes.json's 0.72/0.74): granite-peak/steppe daylight landscapes can carry high dynamic range (bright sky and snow-capped rock against darker steppe vegetation) with no disclosed log/HDR profile confirmed for this specific upload, and no direct visual exposure observation was possible beyond the editorial review's general confirmation the footage was genuine and actively playing. motion_smoothness=0.70 -- scored below this pack's comparable personal-creator exemplars (wanderpeaks' 0.72, dereksturman's 0.74) because the editorial review's planned systematic seeks across 0/25/50/75/95% of runtime (the method this pack otherwise uses to establish continuity/smoothness confidence, e.g. matthiasdangl's five clean full-timeline seeks) were blocked entirely by a recurring browser-profile-lock error before any could be completed, per the task's own instruction to stop rather than retry indefinitely -- so this record has strictly less continuity evidence than its closest comparators, only a general confirmation of genuine, actively-playing vertical drone footage, not confirmation of stable/smooth camera motion specifically; combined with the duration discrepancy above (a second, independent sign this specific video's metadata/analysis chain was less clean than usual for this pack), this is scored at this pack's lower end rather than at parity with its nearest comparators. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.70, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (resolved to weights_version='default-v2', 0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition) via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.7200000000000001 (rounded to 0.72 here). Because `composition` is `None`, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes it from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.74+0.72+0.70)/3 = 0.72 exactly. `editorial_style` is carried over verbatim from this task's supplied editorial-review pass (this authoring session did not itself re-run browser automation against this URL): a live Playwright-controlled Chrome session loaded the actual Short, confirmed genuine vertical drone footage (measured 720x1280, differing from the candidate's yt-dlp-reported 360x640 -- both vertical 9:16, the discrepancy plausibly reflecting format-selection differences between tools rather than a substantive conflict), actively autoplaying, with a real measured HTMLMediaElement.duration of 54.041s -- the discrepancy against the 90s/yt-dlp figure is exactly the tension this record's own `duration` field reasoning above addresses. A recurring browser-profile-lock error ('Browser is already in use ... use --isolated'), which persisted across every follow-up call including browser_close, blocked the planned systematic seeks to 0/25/50/75/95% of runtime; per the task's own instruction to stop rather than retry indefinitely on profile-lock issues, further scrubbing was abandoned once this became clearly blocked. A WebSearch follow-up for creator description or press coverage of this specific video's editing style returned only generic drone-gear/travel/channel-listing results, nothing describing cuts, shot pacing, or transitions for this video. Consequently no genuine observation of cut count, shot length, or transition style was made, and those fields are left null/empty rather than estimated from title/thumbnail/duration alone -- only the format classification (short-form vertical Short) rests on directly-confirmed live-playback data, per the schema's honesty contract; `review_method`='live_playback_review' reflects that a genuine live-playback attempt was made and did load and inspect real page content, not that every planned check (the systematic seeks) succeeded. POST-HOC CORROBORATION found while writing this record: a concurrently-authored sibling exemplar, mikeonthemove-fitzroy-patagonia-granite-peaks.json (a different creator/video, source_url shorts/eZLf4qNdocU, same 'Patagonia-style dramatic granite peak and steppe landscapes' subgenre bucket), documents in its own editorial_style notes that its reviewing session's first navigation attempt to eZLf4qNdocU 'transiently redirected to an unrelated Shorts feed item -- YBMgzLv4kY8' (this record's own video ID), and that its own <video> element read duration=54.041s for eZLf4qNdocU specifically. That is the exact same figure (54.041s) this record's own editorial-review step reported for YBMgzLv4kY8. Given the sibling record's independent, explicit documentation of a live feed-redirect between these two specific video IDs, it is plausible the 54.041s figure attributed to this record's video was actually a cross-contaminated read of eZLf4qNdocU's own <video> element during that redirect, rather than a genuine measurement of YBMgzLv4kY8 itself. This is disclosed as additional, independently-surfaced support for resolving this record's own duration discrepancy in favor of the twice-agreeing yt-dlp figure (90s) over the single live-playback figure (54.041s), per the `duration` field reasoning above -- not merely restated, since this corroborating detail was not available before this sibling file appeared in the working tree mid-session. This record's own filename/source_url (rudywt-patagonia-argentina-granite-steppe.json, YBMgzLv4kY8) is distinct from the sibling's (mikeonthemove-fitzroy-patagonia-granite-peaks.json, eZLf4qNdocU) -- different creator, different specific video -- so no duplicate-record or filename-collision concern applies; both are retained as separate, subgenre-adjacent data points.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live browser check (Playwright) loaded the actual YouTube Short and read the real HTMLMediaElement: confirmed genuine vertical drone footage (720x1280), actively autoplaying, with a real measured duration of 54.041s -- notably different from the 90s duration cited in the candidate's prior yt-dlp verification notes (flagged as a metadata discrepancy worth the parent agent's attention; this record's top-level `duration` field resolves the discrepancy in favor of two independently-agreeing yt-dlp extractions -- see `notes` above). A subsequent browser-profile-lock error ('Browser is already in use ... use --isolated') recurred on every follow-up call (including browser_close), blocking the planned systematic seeks to 0/25/50/75/95% of runtime. Per the task's instruction to stop rather than retry indefinitely on profile-lock issues, further scrubbing was abandoned after this became clearly blocked. WebSearch follow-up for creator description or press coverage of this specific video's editing style returned only generic drone-gear/travel/channel-listing results -- nothing describing cuts, shot pacing, or transitions for this video. Consequently no genuine observation of cut count, shot length, or transition style was possible, and those fields are left null/empty rather than estimated from title/thumbnail/duration alone -- only the format classification (short-form vertical Short) rests on directly-confirmed live playback data.
